name: Gemma 4
archDisplay: Dense Transformer, hybrid local/global attention (31B flagship variant)
note: Most capable open Gemma yet; 31B dense flagship ranks #3 among open models on the Arena AI text leaderboard.
tpsNote: Third-party RTX 4090 tok/s figures found in the wild referenced non-existent Gemma 4 sizes (7B/9B/27B) and were discarded as unreliable.

results.notes: LiveCodeBench v6, 31B dense, also confirmed on huggingface.co/google/gemma-4-31B-it.
Codeforces Elo, 31B dense (26B A4B sibling scores 1718 on the same table, already in corpus).
Humanity's Last Exam, no tools, 31B. Card also lists HLE-with-search 26.5, a distinct tool-augmented setting omitted to avoid conflating with the single 'hle' slug.
Multilingual MMLU, 31B. Slightly above the taxonomy's typical 30-88 range ceiling but confirmed identically on two independent primary sources (ai.google.dev and huggingface.co).

verificationNotes: Confirmed real: released 2026-04-02 (blog.google + deepmind.google, corroborated by independent press). Model card (ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/core/model_card_4, fetched directly) confirms the 31B variant is DENSE with hybrid local/global sliding-window attention (not MoE — a separate 26B A4B MoE variant exists in the family, which is what some press coverage of 'Gemma 4 MoE' refers to), 30.7B total params, 256K context, Apache-2.0 license, and all 3 benchmark numbers (MMLU-Pro 85.2, AIME 2026 89.2, GPQA Diamond 84.3) literally confirmed on that page. hf link google/gemma-4-31B-it confirmed to exist. Arena #3 ranking (1452 Elo) independently corroborated by arena.ai. No changes to record.
